chapters.title: Sovereignty of the People and Supremacy of this Constitution
chapters.swTitle: Mamlaka ya Wananchi na Ukubwa wa Katiba hii
chapters.description: The power belongs to the people of Kenya
chapters.swDescription: Mamlaka ni ya wananchi wa Kenya

sections.title: Sovereignty of the people
sections.originalText: All sovereign power belongs to the people of Kenya and shall be exercised only in accordance with this Constitution.
sections.simplified: All power in Kenya belongs to you, the citizens. Leaders only hold power temporarily on your behalf.
sections.swSimplified: Mamlaka yote nchini Kenya ni yako, mwananchi. Viongozi wanashikilia mamlaka kwa niaba yako tu.
examples: Voting in elections is exercising your sovereign power
Participating in public forums
swExamples: Kupiga kura kwenye uchaguzi ni kutumia mamlaka yako
Kushiriki katika mikutano ya umma

sections.title: Supremacy of this Constitution
sections.originalText: This Constitution is the supreme law of the Republic and binds all persons and all State organs at both levels of government.
sections.simplified: The Constitution is the highest law in the land. No other law, tradition, or person is above it.
sections.swSimplified: Katiba ndiyo sheria kuu ya nchi. Hakuna sheria nyingine, mila, au mtu aliye juu yake.
examples: If a county law contradicts the Constitution, the county law is invalid
swExamples: Ikiwa sheria ya kaunti inapingana na Katiba, sheria hiyo ya kaunti inakuwa batili

chapters.title: The Republic
chapters.description: Kenya as a sovereign state and its values
chapters.swDescription: Kenya kama dola huru na maadili yake

sections.title: Declaration of the Republic
sections.originalText: Kenya is a sovereign Republic... based on democratic values... and multi-party democracy.
sections.simplified: Kenya is an independent, democratic country with multiple political parties.
sections.swSimplified: Kenya ni nchi huru, ya kidemokrasia yenye vyama vingi vya kisiasa.
examples: You have the right to form and join any political party
swExamples: Una haki ya kuanzisha na kujiunga na chama chochote cha kisiasa

sections.title: National Values and Principles of Governance
sections.swTitle: Maadili ya Kitaifa na Kanuni za Utawala
sections.originalText: The national values and principles of governance... bind all State organs, State officers, public officers...
sections.simplified: Kenya is built on values: patriotism, national unity, sharing power, human rights, good governance, integrity, and equality. Government must follow these.
sections.swSimplified: Kenya imejengwa juu ya maadili: uzalendo, umoja wa kitaifa, kugawana mamlaka, haki za binadamu, utawala bora, uadilifu, na usawa. Serikali lazima ifuate haya.
examples: Leaders should serve all people evenly, not just their ethnic group
swExamples: Viongozi wanapaswa kuwatumikia watu wote kwa usawa, si kwa kabila lao tu

chapters.title: Citizenship
chapters.description: Who is a Kenyan citizen and what it means
chapters.swDescription: Nani ni raia wa Kenya na maana yake

sections.title: Citizenship by Birth
sections.originalText: A person is a citizen by birth if at the time of birth, whether or not inside Kenya, either the mother or father of the person was a citizen.
sections.simplified: If either your mother or father was a Kenyan citizen when you were born, you are a Kenyan, anywhere in the world.
sections.swSimplified: Ikiwa mama au baba yako alikuwa raia wa Kenya ulipozaliwa, wewe ni Mkenya, popote duniani.
examples: Child born abroad to a Kenyan parent is Kenyan
swExamples: Mtoto aliyezaliwa nje ya nchi na mzazi Mkenya ni Mkenya

steps: Apply for Kenyan passport or ID
Provide birth certificate and parents' citizenship documents

sections.title: Citizenship by Registration
sections.originalText: A person who has been married to a citizen for a period of at least seven years is entitled on application to be registered as a citizen.
sections.simplified: If you have lived legally in Kenya for 7 years, or are married to a Kenyan for 7 years, you can apply for citizenship.
sections.swSimplified: Ikiwa umeishi kisheria nchini Kenya kwa miaka 7, au umeolewa/kuoa Mkenya kwa miaka 7, unaweza kuomba uraia.
examples: A foreigner married to a Kenyan for 7 years can become a citizen
swExamples: Mgeni aliyeolewa/kuolewa na Mkenya kwa miaka 7 anaweza kuwa raia

sections.title: Dual Citizenship
sections.originalText: A citizen by birth does not lose citizenship by acquiring the citizenship of another country.
sections.simplified: You can be a citizen of Kenya and another country at the same time without losing your Kenyan passport.
sections.swSimplified: Unaweza kuwa raia wa Kenya na nchi nyingine kwa wakati mmoja bila kupoteza pasipoti yako ya Kenya.
examples: You can hold a US passport and a Kenyan passport simultaneously
swExamples: Unaweza kuwa na pasipoti ya Marekani na pasipoti ya Kenya kwa wakati mmoja

chapters.title: The Bill of Rights
chapters.description: Your fundamental rights and freedoms as a Kenyan citizen
chapters.swDescription: Haki zako za kimsingi na kutoingiliwa kama mwananchi wa Kenya

sections.title: Right to Life
sections.originalText: Every person has the right to life. The life of a person begins at conception.
sections.simplified: Every person has the right to life. You cannot be deprived of life intentionally except as authorized by the Constitution.
sections.swSimplified: Kila mtu ana haki ya kuishi. Huwezi kuondolewa maisha kwa makusudi isipokuwa kama ilivyoidhinishwa na Katiba.

sections.title: Equality and Freedom from Discrimination
sections.swTitle: Usawa na Uhuru dhidi ya Ubaguzi
sections.originalText: Every person is equal before the law and has the right to equal protection and equal benefit of the law.
sections.simplified: All Kenyans are equal. Nobody can treat you differently because of tribe, religion, gender, or disability.
sections.swSimplified: Wakenya wote ni sawa. Hakuna mtu anayeweza kukutendea tofauti kwa sababu ya kabila, dini, jinsia, au ulemavu.
examples: A woman cannot be paid less than a man for the same work
Equal access to public services regardless of your home region
swExamples: Mwanamke hawezi kulipwa kidogo kuliko mwanamume kwa kazi ile ile
Ufikiaji sawa wa huduma za umma bila kujali eneo lako

whatToDoIfViolated.contact: Kenya National Commission on Human Rights (KNCHR)

sections.title: Human Dignity
sections.originalText: Every person has inherent dignity and the right to have that dignity respected and protected.
sections.simplified: You deserve to be treated with respect simply because you are human. No one has the right to humiliate you.
sections.swSimplified: Unastahili kutendewa kwa heshima kwa sababu tu wewe ni binadamu. Hakuna mtu aliye na haki ya kukudhalilisha.
examples: Police cannot strip search you in public without following proper procedures
swExamples: Polisi hawawezi kukupekua ukiwa uchi hadharani bila kufuata taratibu sahihi

sections.title: Freedom and Security of the Person
sections.originalText: Every person has the right to freedom and security of the person...
sections.simplified: You cannot be arrested arbitrarily. Your body is yours - no one has the right to torture you or treat you cruelly.
sections.swSimplified: Huwezi kukamatwa kiholela. Mwili wako ni wako - hakuna mtu aliye na haki ya kukutesa au kukutendea kwa ukatili.
examples: Police cannot arrest you without reasonable grounds
Domestic violence is illegal
swExamples: Polisi hawawezi kukukamata bila sababu za msingi
Ukatili wa nyumbani ni kinyume cha sheria

sections.title: Privacy
sections.originalText: Every person has the right to privacy, which includes the right not to have their person, home or property searched...
sections.simplified: Your home, phone, and property are private. Police need a court warrant to search them.
sections.swSimplified: Nyumba yako, simu, na mali yako ni ya faragha. Polisi wanahitaji kibali cha mahakama ili kuvipekua.
examples: Police need a warrant from a judge to search your house or phone
swExamples: Polisi wanahitaji kibali kutoka kwa hakimu ili kupekua nyumba yako au simu yako

whatToDoIfViolated.contact: Office of the Data Protection Commissioner or IPOA
steps: Report to IPOA if police involved

sections.title: Freedom of Expression
sections.originalText: Every person has the right to freedom of expression...
sections.simplified: You have the right to speak your mind, as long as it isn't hate speech or incitement to violence.
sections.swSimplified: Una haki ya kusema unachofikiria, mradi si hotuba ya chuki au uchochezi wa vuravu.
examples: You can criticize the government without fear of arrest
swExamples: Unaweza kuikosoa serikali bila hofu ya kukamatwa

sections.title: Assembly, Demonstration, Picketing and Petition
sections.simplified: You have the right to gather peacefully and protest. You do not need police permission, but you must notify them to provide security.
sections.swSimplified: Una haki ya kukusanyika kwa amani na kuandamana. Huhitaji idhini ya polisi, lakini lazima uwajulishe ili watoe usalama.
examples: Community members protesting poor roads peacefully
swExamples: Wanajamii wakiandamana kwa amani kupinga barabara mbaya

sections.title: Labour Relations
sections.simplified: You have the right to fair pay, reasonable conditions, and to join a trade union. Employers cannot withhold pay.
sections.swSimplified: Una haki ya malipo ya haki, mazingira bora, na kujiunga na chama cha wafanyakazi. Waajiri hawawezi kuzuia malipo.
examples: If your employer hasn't paid you for three months, this is unconstitutional.
swExamples: Ikiwa mwajiri wako hajakulipa kwa miezi mitatu, hii ni kinyume cha katiba.

sections.title: Economic and Social Rights
sections.originalText: Every person has the right to accessible and adequate housing, sanitation, to be free from hunger, safe water, education, and health.
sections.simplified: You have the right to housing, clean water, food, education, and healthcare (including emergency medical treatment).
sections.swSimplified: Una haki ya makazi, maji safi, chakula, elimu, na huduma za afya (pamoja na matibabu ya dharura).
examples: A hospital cannot deny you emergency treatment because you cannot pay the upfront fee
swExamples: Hospitali haiwezi kukukataa matibabu ya dharura kwa sababu huwezi kulipa ada ya awali

sections.title: Rights of an arrested person
sections.simplified: If arrested, you have the right to remain silent, talk to a lawyer, and be brought to court within 24 hours.
sections.swSimplified: Ukikamatwa, una haki ya kukaa kimya, kuzungumza na wakili, na kufikishwa mahakamani ndani ya saa 24.
examples: Police cannot hold you in cells over the weekend indefinitely without bringing you to court on Monday
swExamples: Polisi hawawezi kukuweka ndani kwa muda usiojulikana mwisho wa wiki bila kukufikisha mahakamani Jumatatu

sections.title: Children's Rights
sections.simplified: Children (under 18) have right to care, nutrition, healthcare, education, and protection from abuse.
sections.swSimplified: Watoto (chini ya miaka 18) wana haki ya kutunzwa, lishe, huduma za afya, elimu, na ulinzi dhidi ya unyanyasaji.
examples: All children have the right to free and compulsory basic education
swExamples: Watoto wote wana haki ya kupata elimu ya msingi ya bure na ya lazima

sections.title: Rights of Persons with Disabilities
sections.simplified: Persons with disabilities have right to be treated with dignity, access education, and access to all public places.
sections.swSimplified: Watu wenye ulemavu wana haki ya kutendewa kwa heshima, kupata elimu, na kufika sehemu zote za umma.
examples: A school cannot deny admission to a student due to a physical disability
swExamples: Shule haiwezi kukataa kumsajili mwanafunzi kwa sababu ya ulemavu wa mwili

chapters.title: Land and Environment
chapters.description: Land rights, ownership, and environmental protection
chapters.swDescription: Haki za ardhi, umiliki, na ulinzi wa mazingira

sections.title: Protection of Right to Property
sections.swTitle: Ulinzi wa Haki ya Kumiliki Mali
sections.originalText: Every person has the right, either individually or in association with others, to acquire and own property...
sections.simplified: You have the right to own property. The government cannot take it away arbitrarily, and landlords cannot evict you unlawfully.
sections.swSimplified: Una haki ya kumiliki mali. Serikali haiwezi kuichukua kiholela, na wenye nyumba hawawezi kukufukuza kinyume cha sheria.
examples: A landlord needs a court order to evict you
Government must compensate you fairly if taking your land for a road
swExamples: Mwenye nyumba anahitaji amri ya mahakama ili kukufukuza
Serikali lazima ikulipe fidia ya haki ikiwa inachukua ardhi yako kwa ajili ya barabara

sections.title: Principles of Land Policy
sections.simplified: Land should be owned and used fairly, securely, and sustainably. Elimination of gender discrimination in land ownership.
sections.swSimplified: Ardhi inapaswa kumilikiwa na kutumiwa kwa haki, usalama, na uendelevu. Kukomesha ubaguzi wa kijinsia katika umiliki wa ardhi.
examples: A woman in rural areas can inherit her parents' land just like her brothers
swExamples: Mwanamke katika maeneo ya vijijini anaweza kurithi ardhi ya wazazi wake kama ndugu zake wa kiume

sections.title: Environment
sections.simplified: Government must protect the environment (forests, rivers, wildlife) and ensure equitable sharing of benefits.
sections.swSimplified: Serikali lazima ilinde mazingira (misitu, mito, wanyamapori) na kuhakikisha ugavi wa haki wa faida.
examples: Communities near forests should benefit from the natural resources properly
swExamples: Jamii zilizo karibu na misitu zinapaswa kufaidika na maliasili ipasavyo

sections.title: Responsibilities of leadership
sections.originalText: The authority assigned to a State officer is a public trust to be exercised in a manner that demonstrates respect for the people...
sections.simplified: Leaders work for the people. They must be honest, objective, and accountable. They cannot use their position for personal gain.
sections.swSimplified: Viongozi hufanya kazi kwa ajili ya wananchi. Lazima wawe waaminifu, waadilifu, na wawajibikaji. Hawawezi kutumia nafasi zao kwa faida ya kibinafsi.
examples: A public officer should not be corrupt or ask for bribes
swExamples: Afisa wa umma hapaswi kuwa fisadi au kuomba rushwa

sections.title: Conduct of State officers
sections.simplified: A State officer must behave in public and private life in a way that avoids conflict of interest or demeaning their office.
sections.swSimplified: Afisa wa Serikali lazima ajiendeshe katika maisha ya umma na ya faragha kwa njia inayoepuka mivutano ya kimaslahi au kudhalilisha ofisi yake.

sections.title: General principles for the electoral system
sections.simplified: Elections must be free, fair, secret, free from violence, and transparent.

sections.title: Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission
sections.simplified: The IEBC is completely independent and handles all elections and fair boundary reviews.

sections.title: Role of Parliament
sections.simplified: Parliament represents the will of the people and exercises their sovereignty by making laws.
examples: Your MP introduces a bill in parliament on your behalf

sections.title: Public access and participation
sections.simplified: Parliament MUST involve the public in law-making. They cannot pass major laws entirely in secret.
examples: You can submit a memorandum to parliament when a new bill is proposed

chapters.title: The Executive
chapters.description: The President, Deputy President, and Cabinet

sections.title: Authority of the President
sections.simplified: The President is the Head of State. They must respect, uphold, and safeguard the Constitution.
examples: The President cannot make decisions that override the Constitution

sections.title: Cabinet
sections.simplified: The Cabinet consists of the President, Deputy President, Attorney-General, and 14-22 Cabinet Secretaries.

sections.title: Judicial authority
sections.simplified: Justice shall be done to all, irrespective of status. Justice shall not be delayed.
examples: Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) like mediation is highly encouraged by the Constitution

sections.title: Independence of the Judiciary
sections.simplified: Judges are completely independent. No politician or entity can tell them how to rule.

sections.title: Objectives of devolution
sections.simplified: Devolution brings services closer to you and allows counties to manage their own local affairs and resources.
examples: Your county government handles local hospitals and ECD schools

sections.title: Legislative authority of county assemblies
sections.simplified: Your County Assembly makes local laws regarding health facilities, county roads, agriculture, and local trade.

sections.title: Principles of public finance
sections.simplified: The public finance system must be completely transparent. The public must be involved in budgeting and money matters.
examples: You have the right to attend public participation forums on the county budget

sections.title: Budgets
sections.simplified: Budgets must be open, clear, and state precisely where money comes from and where it is going.

sections.title: Values and principles of public service
sections.simplified: Public service must reflect high standards of ethics, efficient use of resources, and prompt, impartial service to citizens.
examples: A government clerk cannot refuse to serve you because of your ethnic background

sections.title: Principles of national security
sections.simplified: National security organs must respect human rights and fundamentally exist to protect you and your property.

sections.title: Objects of the National Police Service
sections.simplified: The Police must protect life, comply with human rights frameworks, and treat all citizens with respect.

steps: Report police misconduct to the Independent Policing Oversight Authority

chapters.title: Commissions and Independent Offices
chapters.description: Independent oversight bodies (IEBC, KNCHR, JSC, etc)

sections.title: Application of Chapter
sections.simplified: Establishes independent commissions like the Human Rights Commission, Land Commission, and IEBC to protect your rights from government overreach.

sections.title: Amendment by popular initiative
sections.simplified: A proposed amendment affecting territory, fundamental rights, or term of President requires a referendum by the people. The people must always agree.
examples: The BBI initiative required a referendum because it altered the structure of government

sections.title: Construing this Constitution
sections.simplified: The Constitution should be interpreted in a way that respects human rights, promotes its values, and protects the people.

sections.title: Transitional provisions
sections.simplified: This handles how the country safely transitioned into the new 2010 Constitution.
